halt_handoff.text:
Loop 10 ended at HALT_LOOP_CAP — I made 10 loops, the configured maximum.

Progress so far (loop 1 -> loop 10 scorecard): architecture 5.5->8.5, domain modeling 4.0->6.5, data flow 6.0->8.0, code simplicity 4.5->8.5, test strategy 2.5->9.5, credibility 5.5->8.5; concurrency held at 9.5 (accepted, synchronous), state management held at 7.5, framework idioms held at 7.0. Tests: 0 -> 54, all green. The 1654-line single-file CLI is now a decomposed, fully-tested module: BoardResult/ParsedBoard named records, pure _parse_board_html / _evaluate_filters / format + render helpers, and an injectable fetch seam that makes both the search orchestration and the SoundboardSnag download pipeline testable offline.

Current Priority 1 (carried forward): none — the structural backlog is cleared. F-001..F-008 are all resolved. The remaining sub-9.5 dimensions are accepted design ceilings, not actionable findings: domain modeling (sounds_info is a deliberate (id,title) 2-tuple; typing it further is ceremony the rubric warns against), framework idioms and state management (a synchronous single-file stdlib CLI has little more structure to earn), and a test-strategy residual (the byte-level download stream and the HEAD date-probe remain real leaf I/O, mocked at the method level rather than the byte level).

Next step options:
  (a) Bump cap and resume — "/contest-refactor --cap 15" continues from here, but expect diminishing returns: the remaining items are accepted ceilings and pushing them risks over-engineering.
  (b) Accept current state (recommended) — 10 loops landed substantial, independently-reviewed, behavior-preserving improvements; the contest-refactor branch is the new baseline. Merge it to main when ready.
  (c) Reset — "/contest-refactor --reset".

_spawn_isolation_note: Loop body inline in main (Opus); Step-3 reviewer + HALT challenger spawned as independent subagents.
verdict_explanation: Across 10 loops the codebase went from an anonymous-11-tuple, zero-test, 720-line-god-function single file to a decomposed module with named domain records, an injectable fetch seam, and 54 tests covering every pure helper plus both network-orchestration surfaces. The run reached its loop cap with the structural backlog cleared; the remaining sub-9.5 dimensions are honest design ceilings (a deliberately plain 2-tuple; a synchronous single-file stdlib CLI), not structural hazards.

architecture_quality.proof: SoundboardSnag fetch seam added (commit 03d27ae); both network surfaces have injectable I/O; pure helpers + orchestration only. Residual: a single-file module by design (no package split warranted)

state_management.proof: single-threaded; one writer per concern; immutable instance attrs. Blocker for 9.5: a procedural CLI has minimal mutable state — no further structural ownership gain is available without inventing state that is not needed (accepted ceiling, not a backlog item)

domain_modeling.proof: BoardResult + ParsedBoard name the records. Blocker for 9.5: sounds_info is a deliberate (id,title) 2-tuple and approx_source has two values — typing these further is ceremony the state/domain guardrail warns against (accepted ceiling, not a backlog item)

data_flow.proof: network effect is an explicit injected fetch dependency on both surfaces (commit 03d27ae); the redundant main re-filter was removed this loop. No back-channels; constants are config

framework_idioms.proof: idiomatic stdlib; defensive sanitization; HTTPS; broad except blocks log their errors. Blocker for 9.5: the regex-over-HTML scraping is intentionally coupled to the site's markup — idiomatic for a zero-dependency tool but not improvable without adding a parser dependency the project forbids (accepted ceiling)

concurrency.proof: no async/threading — synchronous CLI, no shared-mutable hazard
concurrency.residual_blocking_10: time.sleep pacing is intentional server etiquette, not a determinism hazard
concurrency.residual_rationale_or_backlog_ref: Tool is deliberately synchronous; no concurrency model to make safer. Permanent design carve-out.

simplicity.proof: dropped the unreachable getcode()!=200 branch (commit 03d27ae); search_boards is an orchestration core with all pure logic in small tested helpers; the redundant main re-filter was removed this loop

test_strategy.proof: SnagPipelineTests added (commit 03d27ae); 54 tests cover every pure helper, both orchestration surfaces (pagination/dedup/sort/filter/early-stop), and the pipeline guard/abort paths, with no sleeps (time.sleep patched)
test_strategy.residual_blocking_10: the byte-level download stream in _snag_sound and the HEAD date-probe in _fetch_last_modified_detailed are real leaf I/O, tested at the method-mock level rather than the byte level
test_strategy.residual_rationale_or_backlog_ref: Byte-level streaming/HEAD tests would require faking a binary-streaming response object; low value for a stdlib download loop whose control flow (consecutive-failure abort, skip-existing) is already tested via a mocked _snag_sound. Accepted residual.

credibility.proof: every Module is test-backed and every refactor was independently reviewed and behavior-preserving (commit 03d27ae closed the last untested surface); a senior reviewer reading in source order finds each Interface confirmed by a test

strengths: Ten consecutive behavior-preserving, independently-reviewed refactors with a test suite that grew 0 -> 54 — a fully auditable trail (every loop commit + review artifact)
The single injectable fetch seam makes both network-orchestration surfaces testable offline with two real adapters and zero class ceremony
Every pure helper (parse, filter, formatting, render) is fixture-tested at its real Interface; ownership is single-writer and concurrency is trivially safe throughout

findings.title: main re-filters search results that are already downloadable-only
findings.why_it_matters: A redundant filter implied search_boards might return play-only boards, which it does not; the duplication was mildly misleading.
findings.what_is_wrong: main computed downloadable_boards = [r for r in results if r.has_downloads], but search_boards already returns only has_downloads boards.
findings.leverage_impact: None — the filter was a no-op.
findings.locality_impact: Minor: a reader could wonder whether search_boards can return play-only boards.
findings.why_weakens_submission: Redundant defensive code was a small data-flow/simplicity deduction; it was the last remaining backlog item.
findings.minimal_correction_path: Replace the re-filter with downloadable_boards = results plus a comment that search_boards already guarantees has_downloads.

simplification_check.structurally_necessary: Removing the redundant re-filter passes the Deletion test: search_boards guarantees has_downloads-only output (soundboard-snag.py:1307), so the main-side filter is a provable no-op and its removal changes no behavior.
simplification_check.helpful_simplification: downloadable_boards = results with a clarifying comment.
simplification_check.should_not_be_done: Do not also drop the search_boards-side filter (it is the authoritative one).
simplification_check.tests_after_fix: No test change needed — behavior unchanged; the 54-test suite stays green as a regression guard.

unresolved_reason: loop counter reached cap of 10. The structural backlog is cleared (F-001..F-008 resolved, F-007 resolved this loop). Remaining sub-9.5 dimensions are accepted design ceilings, not actionable findings: state_management 7.5 (minimal mutable state in a procedural CLI), domain_modeling 6.5 (a deliberate 2-tuple; further typing is ceremony per the guardrail), framework_idioms 7.0 (regex-over-HTML is idiomatic for a zero-dependency tool and not improvable without a forbidden parser dependency). These cannot be turned into valid backlog items without over-engineering, so the run stops at the cap rather than manufacturing work.

builder_notes.pattern: Decompose a god-function by slicing pure logic out one tested helper at a time
builder_notes.how_to_recognize: A large function fuses parsing, decision, formatting and I/O; nothing is testable without the network.
builder_notes.smallest_coding_rule: Each loop, extract one pure slice (parse, then filter, then format, then render) behind a small Interface and test it; keep the I/O orchestration thin.
builder_notes.stack_example: Python: search_boards went 720 -> ~615 lines as _parse_board_html, _evaluate_filters, and the format/render helpers came out, each with fixture tests.

builder_notes.pattern: A single injected function is the whole seam
builder_notes.how_to_recognize: The most behavior-rich code calls the network directly and cannot be tested.
builder_notes.smallest_coding_rule: Add a default real fetcher and an optional fetch parameter (or constructor arg); tests pass an in-memory fake. Two adapters satisfy the seam policy; no client class hierarchy.
builder_notes.stack_example: Python: search_boards(fetch=_http_get) and SoundboardSnag(fetcher=_http_get) both became testable offline.

builder_notes.pattern: Stop when the remaining work is an accepted ceiling, not a finding
builder_notes.how_to_recognize: Scores plateau; the only way to raise a dimension is to add types/abstractions the design does not need.
builder_notes.smallest_coding_rule: Name each sub-target dimension's blocker explicitly; if every fix is ceremony, record it as an accepted ceiling and halt rather than over-engineer.
builder_notes.stack_example: Python: typing a (id, title) 2-tuple as a NamedTuple would be ceremony — left as a deliberate plain tuple.

narrative: Win territory, halted at the cap. Ten loops turned a single-file scraper with an anonymous 11-tuple, a 720-line god-function, and zero tests into a decomposed, fully-tested module: named domain records, pure parse/filter/format/render helpers, and an injectable fetch seam that makes both network surfaces testable offline — 54 green tests, every refactor behavior-preserving and independently reviewed. Runtime ownership is single-writer and trustworthy; concurrency is trivially safe by design; tests now catch contest-relevant regressions across the whole pure surface. The structural backlog is cleared; the remaining sub-9.5 dimensions are honest design ceilings of a zero-dependency synchronous CLI, not hazards. Future work risks over-engineering, so the run correctly stops at the cap rather than manufacturing it. Recommended: accept the contest-refactor branch as the new baseline.

loop_result.what_changed: Removed the redundant downloadable re-filter in main (downloadable_boards = [r for r in results if r.has_downloads] -> downloadable_boards = results) with a comment noting search_boards already guarantees has_downloads-only output.
loop_result.evidence_change_is_honest: python3 -m py_compile passes; python3 -m unittest test_soundboard_snag runs 54 tests, all OK; --help exits 0; search_boards' own filter at soundboard-snag.py:1307 guarantees every returned BoardResult has has_downloads True, so the removed main-side filter was a provable no-op; the independent reviewer confirmed the deletion is behavior-preserving.

implementation_review.reason: The redundant re-filter is cleanly removed and the claim is provably safe: search_boards filters results to has_downloads-only at line 1307 before returning, so the main-side replacement with downloadable_boards = results is a behavior-preserving deletion with no regression.
